name: Pear-And-Apricot Tart
recipeIngredient: 1/8 cup Unsalted butter; chilled, cut into small pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the flour, butter, eggs, milk, and vanilla in a food processor fitted with a steel chopping blade.
2. Process until the butter is the size of small peas.
3. Add sugar, salt, and almonds; process just until the dough starts to pull together.
4. Remove the dough from the bowl, pat it into a ball, then flatten it into a disk about 3/4-inch thick.
5. Cover with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 1 hour.
6. Preheat oven to 375F.
7. Gently massage the dough until workable; turn it over a couple of times.
8. Dust dough, rolling pin, and board or pastry cloth with flour.
9. Quickly roll dough into a rectangle large enough to fit into an 11-by-17-inch jelly roll pan and about 1/8-inch thick.
10. If the dough sticks, add small amounts of flour and loosen with a dough scraper or long, thin-bladed knife.
11. Roll the dough over the pin or fold it into quarters and position it over the pan.
12. Unroll, and ease the dough into the tart pan, working to flatten the bottom and ease the dough well into the corners and against the sides.
13. Patch where necessary, then trim any excess dough with a knife.
14. Prick in several places; transfer the pan to the middle of the preheated oven to bake just until the pastry is set but not browned, about 8 to 10 minutes.
15. Remove the pan and let the pastry cool while preparing the filling.
For the Filling:
1. In a medium-sized bowl, combine almond paste, eggs, and amaretto; beat with an electric mixer until smooth.
2. Scrape mixture into the crust.
3. Spread evenly with a metal spatula.
4. Cut pear halves at 1/4-inch intervals to get about 7 slices each.
5. Beginning at the left edge of one long side, place wide end of a pear touching the side of the pan; press gently to elongate it.
6. Put an apricot half at the narrow end.
7. Place an apricot touching the side of the pan, to the right of the first pear, and add a sliced pear with the narrow end touching the apricot.
8. Continue until the whole side is filled with 7 pear halves and 7 apricot halves.
9. Repeat for the second half of the tart, continuing with pears facing in the same direction as the first row.
10. Return the pan to the oven; bake until almond paste is light brown and crust is a rich brown, about 15 to 18 minutes.
11. While the tart bakes, melt jelly over low heat.
12. Remove baked tart, brush with jelly glaze, and let cool to room temperature.
13. Cut into serving portions of an apricot and pear on each slice.
14. Combine yogurt with sour cream and spoon over the tart as it is served.
